question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R6 is reconfigured under a different BGP autonomous system: `router bgp
65000` becomes `router bgp 65001`. Both iBGP sessions (to R3 and R7)
now look like eBGP from R6's side (remote-as 65000, local-as 65001)
but the RRs still treat R6 as iBGP (remote-as 65000). OPEN fails on
every peer-up. R6 is effectively partitioned from the iBGP mesh.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.106 (R6), the BGP process was moved from AS 65000 to AS 65001 (router bgp 65001) while the RRs (R3, R7) still treat R6 as iBGP in AS 65000.
R6 now advertises itself as AS 65001, but BGP requires the AS number a peer advertises to match the remote-as the other end expects; R3 and R7 expect 65000, so the negotiation is rejected as an AS mismatch and neither the R6<->R3 nor the R6<->R7 session can establish, leaving R6 partitioned from the iBGP mesh and no routes exchanged with it.
Fix: restore R6's BGP process under AS 65000 (with its original router-id, redistribute connected, and the R3/R7 neighbors).
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.6.
- R7: neighbor 192.0.2.6.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-ASYNC_IPC_STATUS reported by R6 (3 instances):
- R6: bpm-default:(A)inst-id 0, Connection Closed (Unconfig)
- R6: bpm-default:(A)inst-id 0, Connection Open
- R6: default, process instance 1:(A)inst-id 0, Connection Established

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65001
 bgp router-id 192.0.2.6
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  redistribute connected
 exit
 neighbor 192.0.2.3
  remote-as 65000
  update-source Loopback0
  address-family ipv4 unicast
   route-policy PASS in
   route-policy PASS out
  exit
 exit
 neighbor 192.0.2.7
  remote-as 65000
  update-source Loopback0
  address-family ipv4 unicast
   route-policy PASS in
   route-policy PASS out

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
 bgp router-id 192.0.2.6
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  redistribute connected
 exit
 neighbor 192.0.2.3
  remote-as 65000
  update-source Loopback0
  address-family ipv4 unicast
   route-policy PASS in
   route-policy PASS out
  exit
 exit
 neighbor 192.0.2.7
  remote-as 65000
  update-source Loopback0
  address-family ipv4 unicast
   route-policy PASS in
   route-policy PASS out

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 21:56:31.577 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.6 Down - Peer closing down the session (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 21:56:31.537 UTC: bpm[1099]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ASYNC_IPC_STATUS : bpm-default:(A)inst-id 0, Connection Closed (Unconfig)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 21:56:33.547 UTC: bpm[1099]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ASYNC_IPC_STATUS : bpm-default:(A)inst-id 0, Connection Open
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 21:56:33.748 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ASYNC_IPC_STATUS : default, process instance 1:(A)inst-id 0, Connection Established
